item.title: Swiss Inflation Jumps to Fastest in a Year on Iran War Impact
item.description: Switzerland’s inflation rate jumped in March to the quickest pace in a year as the energy supply crunch caused by the war in the Middle East stoked the cost of heating oil.

item.title: Australia Broadens Fuel Tax Cut in Attempt to Shield Consumers
item.description: Australia will further cut fuel taxes to cushion households from rising prices as the Iran war upends global supply chains.

item.title: FTSE 100 Futures, Pound Fall on War Escalation Worries
Asian Nations Jockey For Leverage to Re-Open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Countries across Asia are jockeying for leverage and adopting splintering approaches as they respond to US President Donald Trump’s call to open the Strait of Hormuz by themselves, amid mounting economic disruptions.

item.title: CEO of Finnish Chocolate Maker Fazer Sees IPO Happening by 2029
item.description: Finnish chocolate maker Fazer is on track for a possible initial public offering within a few years to fund an international expansion and potential acquisitions, Chief Executive Officer Christoph Vitzthum said.

item.title: Barclays Private Bank Singapore Head to Exit Despite Growth Push
item.description: Barclays Plc’s head of private bank in Singapore is leaving the firm just as it prepares to make a full return to the business in the city-state this year.

item.title: Persian Gulf’s Aluminum Prospects Clouded by War, Says Goldman
item.description: War in the Persian Gulf has clouded its prospects as an important source of additional aluminum supply in coming years, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: Canada Plugs Nation as ‘Reliable’ Energy Partner as Oil Soars
item.description: Canada’s top trade official plugged his nation as a stable and reliable energy supplier amid the latest jump in oil prices as President Donald Trump said military operations on Iran could escalate, potentially prolonging disruptions to oil flows through the vital Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: US Naphtha Exports Surge as Trump’s Military Actions Upend Trade
item.description: US naphtha exports are surging as the Iran war cuts off supplies from the Mideast, prompting buyers in Japan to turn to Texas and Louisiana for the petrochemical feedstock.

item.title: China’s Innolight Is Said to Confidentially File for $3 Billion Hong Kong Listing
item.description: Chinese optics firm Zhongji Innolight Co. has confidentially filed for a Hong Kong listing, according to people familiar with the development, in what could be one of the city’s biggest maiden share sales this year.

item.title: Alibaba Unveils Third Closed-Source AI Model in Focus on Profit
item.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. has released its third proprietary AI model in as many days, reinforcing the company’s intent to focus on profiting off its flagship artificial intelligence services.

item.title: Private Equity Sales Slump as AI, War Bring New Stress Fractures
item.description: Private equity sales have fallen by more than a third this year, as developments in artificial intelligence and war in Iran heap pressure on a subdued exit market.

item.title: Japan 10-Year Sovereign Bond Sale Sees Weakest Demand Since May
item.description: Japan’s 10-year government bond auction saw the weakest demand since May after a renewed rise in oil prices fueled inflation worries and damped investor appetite.

item.title: India Trading Ban Rocks $149 Billion-a-Day Offshore Rupee Market
item.description: India banned its banks from offering the most popular instrument for trading the rupee offshore, threatening to squeeze a $149 billion-a-day market in an extreme step to shore up its tumbling currency.

item.title: Treasuries Fall as Trump’s Iran Threats Add to Inflation Concern
item.description: Treasuries dropped across the curve after President Donald Trump’s threatening tone toward Iran in a prime-time address pushed up oil prices and added to concern over inflation.

item.title: China’s Industrial Hub Eyes Coal, Nuclear As Iran War Upends Gas
item.description: China’s southern industrial hub of Guangdong has asked local power producers to rebuild coal stockpiles, curb the use of gas and accelerate the addition of nuclear generation, in a sign that even the world’s second-largest economy is beginning to feel the impact of war in the Middle East.

item.title: Metals Fall as Trump Repeats Threat to Attack Iran Power Plants
item.description: Copper fell with other industrial metals after US President Donald Trump reiterated a threat to attack Iran’s civilian infrastructure should negotiations fail to end the month-long war.

item.title: RBI’s Latest Attempt to Shield Rupee May Bring Near-Term Rebound
item.description: But will it stick?

item.title: Mexico Budget Plan Sees Smaller Fiscal Gap, More Growth in 2027
item.description: Mexico’s government forecasts a narrower budget deficit next year, even as President Claudia Sheinbaum pushes for increased public spending and new private investment to counter an economic slowdown.

item.title: Trump’s Renewed Iran Warning Risks More Volatility, Analysts Say
item.description: Stocks fell and the dollar rose as markets stayed jittery after President Donald Trump warned the US would hit Iran “extremely hard” within two to three weeks, disappointing traders who had hoped for clearer signs of an end to the war.

item.title: China Drains Cash From Economy in Rare Pullback During Oil Shock
item.description: China’s central bank withdrew cash from its financial system for the first time in a year, a cautious signal that keeps its policy options open as higher oil prices filter through the economy.

item.title: Former BOJ Chief Economist Sees Likelihood of April Rate Hike
item.description: The war in Iran is raising upside risks for inflation, bolstering the case for the Bank of Japan to raise interest rates as soon as this month, according to a former BOJ chief economist.

item.title: Fund Beating 96% of Peers Says Buy Korean Chip Stocks Hit by War
item.description: South Korean memory-chip stocks are still cheap even after the recent rally and are likely to among the biggest gainers on any further signs of deescalation in the Middle East, according to a top-performing fund.

item.title: Malaysia’s Anwar Urges Crackdown on Fake News Over Fuel Prices
item.description: Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim called on enforcement agencies to step up action against what his government sees as false claims about fuel and electricity prices as it seeks to shore up public confidence amid a global energy crunch.

item.title: Meet the New AI Coworker Who Won’t Stop Snitching to Your Boss
item.description: The Slack messages began arriving at 5:47 a.m. on a recent Monday. Three sales proposals had gone out the previous week and none of the team members had scheduled follow-ups. The reminders were crisp, professional and relentless — and they hadn’t been sent by a human.

item.title: UBS Says Dollar-Yen May Rise to 175 on Extended Oil Disruption
item.description: The yen’s slide is seen continuing even as Japanese officials ramp up intervention rhetoric, according to UBS Group AG strategists, who see the dollar-yen pair reaching 175 by year-end in an “extended disruption” scenario.

item.title: Foreigners Chase Malaysian Bonds as War Hits Emerging Markets
item.description: Global investors are pouring money into Malaysian bonds as the Iran conflict drives oil prices higher, bolstering the outlook for the energy-exporting nation while rattling other emerging-market peers.

item.title: China’s Worst Tech Earnings in Three Years Signal Tough Comeback
item.description: Chinese technology companies reported their weakest quarterly profit growth in three years, leaving more doubt for investors looking for a recovery in sector stocks.

item.title: Leveraged SK Hynix ETF Tops Global Inflows Over Tesla, Microsoft
item.description: A leveraged fund targeting SK Hynix Inc. has drawn the most inflows among its peers this year, highlighting persistent bets on the South Korean AI chipmaker’s fortunes following a steep selloff.

item.title: Japan Credit Pipeline Slows as Investors Grapple With Volatility
item.description: The pipeline for Japanese corporate bonds is the slowest since 2023 as investors contend with uncertainty about the Middle East war.
